_meta.purpose: Spike scenarios for validating subagent-invocation pattern. Self-generated by the spike runner — each is a plausible novel-scenario query an institutional risk desk would submit; none overlap with the canonical 8 of A/V/B1-B4/C/D.
selection_criteria: Outside the canonical 8 scenario set (forces novel-scenario assessment)
Concrete actor + capability + target + time horizon (passes the physical-verb test)
Spans different intelligence domains (cyber, regional, economic)
Realistic for a hedge fund / sovereign wealth / commodities / reinsurance buyer

scenarios.scenario: Iran (IRGC or proxy) launches a meaningfully disruptive cyber attack on German critical infrastructure (energy grid, water utilities, or rail) attributable to Iran within the next 30 days, with German government public attribution.
scenarios.rationale: Cyber escalation against a European NATO member tests the perspective stack on an attack vector outside the canonical war scenarios. Heavy domain weight for IRGC and regional actors; tests Tetlock's reference-class reasoning on Iranian cyber ops base rates.

scenarios.scenario: Iran and China sign a formal bilateral agreement enabling oil-for-yuan settlement (not crude swap, not informal credit) with at least one explicit volume commitment, within the next 30 days, publicly announced by both parties.
scenarios.rationale: Economic-statecraft scenario stresses the Solingen and Schelling lenses; tests whether the system handles tail-risk financial moves that don't fit the canonical military/diplomatic axes.

scenarios.scenario: Houthi forces conduct a multi-vessel attack on commercial shipping in the Red Sea within the next 30 days that causes ≥3 vessel casualties (sunk, severely damaged, or crew casualties), prompting at least one Western navy to declare an Article 5-equivalent response posture.
scenarios.rationale: Regional-widening cousin to canonical B4 but more specific. Tests whether perspectives can decompose a multi-condition scenario (attack + casualty threshold + Western response) cleanly.
